# FERC Audit Explorer

> Machine-readable findings of noncompliance and staff recommendations mined from **utility audit and prudence records**, across three collections: **FERC audits** (the Office of Enforcement's financial (FA) and non-financial (PA) audits of utilities, ISOs/RTOs, and pipelines, electric/gas/oil), **FERC prudence reviews** (rate-case cost determinations), and **state PUC/PSC/SCC audits** (rate, fuel-cost, and management proceedings from several state commissions). FERC audits and the parsed state management audits are broken into findings -> recommendations (verbatim); legal orders, testimony, and settlements are listed with their source. Themes are mined by transparent keyword tagging, never LLM judgement.

Scope: FERC utility audits — electric, gas & oil, extended to prudence reviews and state audits. As of 2026-06-10, the corpus holds 294 structured records: 120 FERC audits (of 120 listed at https://www.ferc.gov/audits (233 electric, 36 gas, 18 oil identified)), 27 prudence reviews, and 36 state PUC audits. Findings and recommendations are quoted verbatim. Independent public-interest tool, not affiliated with FERC. Links below are relative to the site root.

## Data (machine-readable)
- [Reports](data/reports.json): every structured record with findings, recommendations, and themes
- [Patterns](data/patterns.json): cross-report themes with counts by industry and year
- [Patterns by collection](data/patterns_by_collection.json): the same aggregates, per collection/tab
- [Meta](data/meta.json): corpus counts and provenance
- [Full corpus text](llms-full.txt): all findings, recommendations, and source notes in one file (verbatim)
- [LLM context](llms-ctx.txt): the same corpus in the llms.txt XML context shape (`<project>`/`<doc>`)

## FERC Audits
_120 document(s), 2014–2025. 584 findings / 1419 recommendations parsed from 120 report(s). top themes: Accounting misclassification, Form reporting (Form No. 1/2/6, Page 700), Depreciation, Below-the-line costs (lobbying, charitable, etc.)._

- [Kern River Gas Transmission Company](https://elibrary.ferc.gov/eLibrary/filelist?accession_number=20250929-3000&optimized=false): Docket FA23-10-000, issued 2025-09-29, financial audit, n/a, 5 finding(s) — findings: Renewable Natural Gas Quality Specifications; Tariff Administration and Oversight; Informational Postings; Allowance for Funds Used During Construction; Annual Membership Dues

## Prudence Reviews
_27 document(s), 2013–2025. jurisdictions: FERC (14), WA (3), GA (3), WI (2), CA (2), IN (1), VA (1), IL (1). 27 listed for reference (orders / testimony / settlements). doc types: ALJ initial decision (5), Commission order on formal challenge (3), fuel cost reconciliation prudence review — Final Decision (2), ERRA compliance & reasonableness review decision (2), Vogtle construction-monitoring report (semi-annual cost/schedule prudence monitoring) (2), order on Schedule 95 Power Cost Adjustment Clause revisions (power-cost prudence/rate order) (1), gas cost adjustment (GCA) order — gas-cost prudence/recovery review (1), power cost adjustment (PCA) prudence-review order (1), fuel factor order (fuel-cost recovery review) (1), Commission order on rehearing (1), Commission order on wholesale power contracts (1), Commission order on complaint (1), Commission order on remand (1), PGA / gas-adjustment-charge reconciliation prudence order (§9-220) (1), policy & interpretive statement on LDC natural-gas hedging practices (compliance/prudence review) (1), Commission opinion on initial decision (1), Commission order on abandonment cost recovery (1), PSC Staff & Independent Construction Monitor prudence testimony (Vogtle 22nd VCM report) (1)._

- [Puget Sound Energy](https://www.utc.wa.gov/casedocket/2025/250747/docsets): WA · order on Schedule 95 Power Cost Adjustment Clause revisions (power-cost prudence/rate order), Docket UE-250747, issued 2025-12-24 — listed for reference
- [Southern Indiana Gas and Electric Company d/b/a CenterPoint Energy Indiana South](https://www.in.gov/oucc/natural-gas/tips-and-publications/gas-cost-adjustments/): IN · gas cost adjustment (GCA) order — gas-cost prudence/recovery review, Docket Cause No. 37366 GCA 168, issued 2025-10-29 — listed for reference
- [Puget Sound Energy](https://www.utc.wa.gov/casedocket/2025/250318/docsets): WA · power cost adjustment (PCA) prudence-review order, Docket UE-250318, issued 2025-09-29 — listed for reference

## State PUC Audits
_36 document(s), 2017–2025. jurisdictions: PA (10), MI (4), NJ (4), NY (3), MS (2), CA (2), UT (2), OH (2), MO (2), CT (2), TN (1), MD (1), IL (1). 109 findings / 311 recommendations parsed from 10 report(s). 26 listed for reference (orders / testimony / settlements). doc types: utilities staff annual report (2), balancing accounts performance audit (Utility Audits Branch) (2), energy balancing account audit report (2), distribution reliability audit — Part Two (programs & operations) (2), distribution reliability audit — Part One (infrastructure) (2), comprehensive management & operations audit (PSC order releasing audit report, PSL 66(19)) (2), staff prudence review report (2), management efficiency investigation (1), weather normalization adjustment audit (1), order initiating gas-system safety-inspection investigation (documents PSC Engineering Division audit findings) (1), affiliate-transactions audit & management audit, Phase Two (Liberty Consulting) (1), rider audit report (Daymark Energy Advisors, PUCO third-party audit of Rider DMR) (1), independent investigative audit (Blue Ridge Consulting, PUCO-ordered) (1), affiliate-transactions, operational/financial performance & management audit (Liberty Consulting) (1), ICC investigation order incorporating Liberty management audit (gas System Modernization Program) (1), performance investigation final decision (1), rate design investigation decision (1), DPS Office of Investigations & Enforcement investigation report (customer billing system) (1), affiliate-transactions audit & comprehensive management audit (1). top themes: Customer service & billing, Workforce, training & succession planning, Internal audit & internal controls, Inventory, materials & fleet._

- [New Jersey Natural Gas Company](https://www.nj.gov/bpu/about/divisions/audits/auditreports.html): NJ · affiliate-transactions audit & comprehensive management audit, Docket GA13010008, issued n/a — listed for reference

## Common themes (from parsed findings)
- Accounting misclassification — Costs or revenues booked to the wrong FERC account. (67 report(s), 180 finding(s))
- Form reporting (Form No. 1/2/6, Page 700) — Errors or omissions in the annual FERC forms utilities file. (57 report(s), 152 finding(s))
- Depreciation — Unapproved or incorrect depreciation rates applied to plant. (46 report(s), 66 finding(s))
- Below-the-line costs (lobbying, charitable, etc.) — Non-recoverable costs (lobbying, charity, ads) charged to ratepayers. (40 report(s), 50 finding(s))
- Property & plant records — Incomplete or inaccurate utility plant and property records. (39 report(s), 69 finding(s))
- AFUDC / cost of capital — Mis-stated AFUDC — the financing cost capitalized during construction. (37 report(s), 47 finding(s))
- Cost of service & rates — Errors in rate-base or return inputs to cost-of-service rates. (35 report(s), 73 finding(s))
- Affiliate / intercompany transactions — Transactions with affiliated companies mis-priced or mis-reported. (35 report(s), 53 finding(s))
- Tariff administration & oversight — Not following the utility's own FERC-approved tariff. (29 report(s), 49 finding(s))
- Capitalization vs. expense — Costs capitalized that should be expensed, or the reverse. (20 report(s), 21 finding(s))
- Service reliability & vegetation management — Electric reliability (SAIDI/CAIDI), outages, or vegetation-management programs. (12 report(s), 14 finding(s))
- Internal audit & internal controls — Weak internal controls, compliance programs, or internal-audit coverage. (11 report(s), 24 finding(s))
- Inventory, materials & fleet — Inventory accuracy, materials management, or fleet/vehicle management gaps. (11 report(s), 16 finding(s))
- Membership dues & industry associations — Trade-association dues (e.g., EEI) improperly charged to ratepayers. (11 report(s), 11 finding(s))
- Customer service & billing — Customer-service performance, billing accuracy, or call-center issues. (10 report(s), 14 finding(s))
- Workforce, training & succession planning — Staffing, training, span-of-control, or leadership-succession shortcomings. (9 report(s), 22 finding(s))
- Informational postings — Required public postings (e.g., OASIS) missing, late, or incomplete. (9 report(s), 15 finding(s))
- Information technology & systems — IT governance, systems, budgets, or project-management deficiencies. (9 report(s), 10 finding(s))
- Emergency preparedness & business continuity — Weak emergency response, storm readiness, or business-continuity planning. (7 report(s), 8 finding(s))
- Cybersecurity & physical security — Gaps in cyber defenses or physical security of facilities and systems. (5 report(s), 8 finding(s))
- Dividend policy & capital management — Dividend-policy or capital-management practices flagged by auditors. (5 report(s), 5 finding(s))
- Gas safety & pipeline integrity — Gas leak backlogs, corrosion control, or pipeline-integrity practices. (3 report(s), 4 finding(s))
- Creditworthiness — Customer credit standards not applied as the tariff requires. (3 report(s), 3 finding(s))

## Optional
- [Human-readable explorer](index.html)
- [FERC audit reports (primary source)](https://www.ferc.gov/audits)
